title: The Tree of Samsara & Yoga
summary: The cosmic tree of creation and the practice of Yoga to still the senses and realize Brahman. Addresses spiritual practice, focus, and ultimate peace.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “With its root above and branches below stands this Banyan-tree. That (root) is indeed the Pure, that is Brahman, the Immortal. All the worlds are contained in It and no one goes beyond It.…” and develops toward “Instructed by Yama , in the knowledge, in this manner, as also in the technique of Yoga Sastra, Nachiketa became sinless and transcended physical…”

verses.sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वमूलोऽवाक्शाख एषोऽश्वत्थः सनातनः । तदेव शुक्रं तद्ब्रह्म तदेवामृतमुच्यते । तस्मिँल्लोकाः श्रिताः सर्वे तदु नात्येति कश्चन । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.translation: With its root above and branches below stands this Banyan-tree. That (root) is indeed the Pure, that is Brahman, the Immortal. All the worlds are contained in It and no one goes beyond It. This is verily That (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit: यदिदं किं च जगत् सर्वं प्राण एजति निःसृतम् । महद्भयं वज्रमुद्यतं य एतद्विदुरमृतास्ते भवन्ति ॥
verses.translation: The whole world, whatever there is, springs from and moves in Prana (Brahman), which is a great terror like an upraised thunder-bolt. Those who know This become deathless.

verses.sanskrit: भयादस्याग्निस्तपति भयात्तपति सूर्यः । भयादिन्द्रश्च वायुश्च मृत्युर्धावति पञ्चमः ॥
verses.translation: The fire burns, the sun shines and Indra , the Wind God and the God of Death - the fifth in row - perform their tasks, all out of fear for Him.

verses.sanskrit: इह चेदशकद्बोद्धुं प्राक्षरीरस्य विस्रसः । ततः सर्गेषु लोकेषु शरीरत्वाय कल्पते ॥
verses.translation: If one is not able to realise ( Him ) before his body falls, he becomes liable to be embodied again in the future creations.

verses.sanskrit: यथाऽऽदर्शे तथाऽऽत्मनि यथा स्वप्ने तथा पितृलोके । यथाऽप्सु परीव ददृशे तथा गन्धर्वलोके छायातपयोरिव ब्रह्मलोके ॥
verses.translation: In one's own soul Brahman is realised clearly, as if seen in a mirror. In the world of the ancestors He is perceived as a dream (hazily); in the world of angels as a reflection in water and in the world of Brahma as light distinct from shade.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणां पृथग्भावमुदयास्तमयौ च यत् । पृथगुत्पद्यमानानां मत्वा धीरो न शोचति ॥
verses.translation: The wise one, having understood the individual nature of the senses, their rising and setting as of things that come into being separate from the soul, does not grieve.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनो मनसः सत्त्वमुत्तमम् । सत्त्वादधि महानात्मा महतोऽव्यक्तमुत्तमम् ॥
verses.translation: Mind is greater than the senses, the discriminating power (Intellect) is Greater than the mind , the Great Brahman is higher than the intellect and the unmanifested is greater than the Atman.

verses.sanskrit: अव्यक्तात्तु परः पुरुषो व्यापकोऽलिङ्ग एव च । यं ज्ञात्वा मुच्यते जन्तुरमृतत्वं च गच्छति ॥
verses.translation: The Purusha is beyond the unmanifested , is all-pervading and attributeless , realising That, a creature is liberated and attains to deathlessness.

verses.sanskrit: न संदृशे तिष्ठति रूपमस्य न चक्षुषा पश्यति कश्चनैनम् । हृदा मनीषा मनसाऽभिक्लृप्तो य एतद्विदुरमृतास्ते भवन्ति ॥
verses.translation: None beholds Him with the eyes, for He is without visible form. He is revealed in the heart through self-control and meditation. Those who know Him become immortal.

verses.sanskrit: यदा पञ्चावतिष्ठन्ते ज्ञानानि मनसा सह । बुद्धिश्च न विचेष्टते तामाहुः परमां गतिम् ॥
verses.translation: When the five senses of perception stand still along with the mind, and when the intellect does not move, that is called the Supreme State.

verses.sanskrit: तां योगमिति मन्यन्ते स्थिरामिन्द्रियधारणाम् । अप्रमत्तस्तदा भवति योगो हि प्रभवाप्ययौ ॥
verses.translation: This steadiness of the mind and senses is what is known as Yoga. One becomes watchful at that time, for Yoga is subject to growth and decay.

verses.sanskrit: नैव वाचा न मनसा प्राप्तुं शक्यो न चक्षुषा । अस्तीति ब्रुवतोऽन्यत्र कथं तदुपलभ्यते ॥
verses.translation: The Atman cannot be attained by speech, by the mind or by the eye. How can It be realised otherwise than from those who know It as existing?
He is to be realised as "existing", and then that It has a transcendental nature. His innermost nature reveals itself to those who apprehend It as existing.

verses.sanskrit: यदा सर्वे प्रमुच्यन्ते कामा येऽस्य हृदि श्रिताः । अथ मर्त्योऽमृतो भवत्यत्र ब्रह्म समश्नुते ॥
verses.translation: The mortal gains immortality and in this life becomes Brahman, when all the yearnings in his mind are dissolved.

verses.sanskrit: यदा सर्वे प्रभिद्यन्ते हृदयस्येह ग्रन्थयः । अथ मर्त्योऽमृतो भवत्येतावद्ध्यनुशासनम् ॥
verses.translation: In this body, when all those knots in the mind are cut , then the mortal becomes immortal ; thus runs the instruction (in Vedic scriptures).

verses.sanskrit: शतं चैका च हृदयस्य नाड्य- स्तासां मूर्धानमभिनिःसृतैका । तयोर्ध्वमायन्नमृतत्वमेति विष्वङ्ङन्या उत्क्रमणे भवन्ति ॥
verses.translation: Of the hundred and one are nerves of the heart; one extends towards the top (crown) of the head. Proceeding upwards through it, man attains deathlessness and the others lead one to different destinations (human or non-human form).

verses.sanskrit: अङ्गुष्ठमात्रः पुरुषोऽन्तरात्मा सदा जनानां हृदये संनिविष्टः । तं स्वाच्छरीरात्प्रवृहेन्मुञ्जादिवेषीकां धैर्येण । तं विद्याच्छुक्रममृतं तं विद्याच्छुक्रममृतमिति ॥
verses.translation: In the heart of everybeing dwells, the inner soul, of the dimension of the human thumb , The Purusha, whom through meditation and persverance is like drawing out a thread from the stalk. Him one should know as pure and immortal. Him one should know as pure and immortal.

verses.sanskrit: मृत्युप्रोक्तां नचिकेतोऽथ लब्ध्वा विद्यामेतां योगविधिं च कृत्स्नम् । ब्रह्मप्राप्तो विरजोऽभूद्विमृत्यु- रन्योऽप्येवं यो विदध्यात्ममेव ॥
verses.translation: Instructed by Yama , in the knowledge, in this manner, as also in the technique of Yoga Sastra, Nachiketa became sinless and transcended physical death and attained Brahman. Also any other will do so , on realising the Brahman.
